params.description: 3×6 array appears. 3 rows, 6 items per row, 18 dots total.

beats.text: Two different ways to prove the same thing. Highlighting: you read the same array two ways. Rotation: you turn the array and rows become columns. Either way, same dots, same product.

params.description: Rows highlight sequentially. 3 rows, 6 items each. 3 × 6 = 18.
Columns highlight sequentially. 6 columns, 3 items each. 6 × 3 = 18.
Array rotates 90 degrees. 3×6 becomes 6×3. Same 18 dots.
Array now shows 6 rows, 3 items per row. Same 18 dots.

beats.text: This doesn't just work for these arrays. It works for EVERY array. Any array you can build. The two expressions will always give the same product. Always.
Why do both expressions always give the same product?
options: Arrays are always the same size
Multiplication always gives the same answer

beats.text: Same dots. Whether you read them by rows or columns, whether you turn the array, the dots don't change. That's why the product can't change.

elements.description: 6×3 array displayed. 6 rows, 3 items per row, 18 dots total.
